title: Detect empty/zero-token API responses and surface error (not silent freeze)
description:
## Problem (HIGH IMPACT — root cause of recurring 'freeze' reports)
When the model provider returns an EMPTY response (zero tokens, empty content, stop_reason=None), pi records it as a successful empty turn and idles. The user sees a 'freeze' and types 'resume' repeatedly — each retry sends the same request, gets the same empty response, and the turn ends instantly with zero feedback.

CONFIRMED in production (MiniMax-M2.7, print_designer session 019f6e59):
- Token timeline: 82415 -> 83075 -> 83222 -> 83778 -> 83967 -> 0 -> 0 -> 0 -> 0
- Model worked fine for 38s, then PERMANENT cliff of zero-token responses
- Quota was NOT exhausted (h5: 10%, weekly: 17%) — API was failing (auth expiry or poisoned payload)
- Each empty response: usage={input:0,output:0,totalTokens:0}, content=[], stop_reason=None, finish_reason=None
- User typed 'resume' 3x over 4 minutes — all returned empty, zero feedback

The Role Model surfaces API errors and retries with backoff / model fallback. pi-harness currently treats zero-token responses as valid empty turns.

## Detection signature
In message_end handler (index.ts), flag a response as 'empty-failure' when ALL hold:
- usage.totalTokens === 0  (or usage.output === 0)
- message.content is empty ([]) or whitespace-only
- stop_reason is null/undefined
- message.role === 'assistant'

## Fix
1. Add an emptyResponseCounter in index.ts (like outputLimitResumeAttempts)
2. On detecting an empty-failure response:
   - Increment counter
   - If counter < threshold (e.g., 2): auto-retry with a 'previous response was empty, please respond' nudge
   - If counter >= threshold: surface a clear ctx.ui.notify ERROR: 'Model is returning empty responses (API/auth issue). Switch models with /model or re-authenticate.'
3. Reset counter on any non-empty response

```ts
// in message_end handler, before the output-limit check:
const isEmptyFailure =
    m.usage?.totalTokens === 0 &&
    Array.isArray(event.message.content) &&
    event.message.content.length === 0 &&
    !m.stopReason;
if (isEmptyFailure) {
    emptyResponseCount += 1;
    if (emptyResponseCount >= EMPTY_RESPONSE_ALERT_THRESHOLD) {
        ctx.ui.notify(
            `Model returned ${emptyResponseCount} empty responses in a row — API/auth failure, not a freeze. Switch models (/model) or re-auth.`,
            'error',
        );
        return; // stop auto-resuming into a dead API
    }
    queueAutoResume('empty-response-retry', 'The previous response was empty. Please respond to the last message.', 'followUp');
}
```

## Why priority 1
This is the ACTUAL root cause of the user's recurring 'pi freezes' reports — not compaction, not output limits, not context window. Those are secondary. Empty-response swallowing makes every provider hiccup look like a permanent freeze.

## Files
- EDIT: `index.ts` (message_end handler: add empty-response detection)
- NEW: const EMPTY_RESPONSE_ALERT_THRESHOLD = 2

## Acceptance
- 2+ consecutive zero-token responses -> clear error notification (not silent idle)
- Auto-retry once on first empty response
- Counter resets on real response
- Notification names the real cause ('API/auth failure') so user switches models instead of typing 'resume' forever
notes:
## USER FEEDBACK: must show actionable diagnostic ("Login again"), not just detect empty
User point: at least show a message like Login again or something.

Confirmed credential structure (auth.json):
- minimax, zai, openrouter: STATIC API key (type+key only, NO expiry) -> cannot expire on a timer
- openai-codex: OAuth (access+refresh+expires) -> DOES expire; currently ~85h left

### Provider-aware messaging requirement (extends the detection fix)
1. OAUTH providers (openai-codex): on empty/failure -> ATTEMPT token refresh using refresh token FIRST (transparent retry); if refresh fails -> notify "Session expired - Login again (/login or re-authenticate <provider>)". Optionally proactively refresh before expiry.
2. STATIC-key providers (minimax/zai/openrouter): cannot be TTL-expired. On repeated empty/failure -> notify "Provider <X> is rejecting requests (API key invalid/revoked or service issue). Switch models with /model or verify your key." Do NOT say 'login again' (misleading for static keys).
3. Detection signature unchanged: usage.totalTokens===0 && content===[] && stop_reason===null; after N=2 consecutive -> surface provider-specific message.

### Impact
MiniMax empty-response cliff was PERMANENT (4+ min of zeros) with ZERO feedback. User typed 'resume' repeatedly into a dead endpoint. Any provider-aware message would have unblocked in seconds.

### Files
- EDIT: index.ts message_end handler (empty detection + provider credential-type lookup)
- NEW: helper reading auth.json to classify provider as static-key vs oauth and pick message
- NEW: for oauth providers, call existing token-refresh path before notifying

## BREAKTHROUGH: root cause found via direct API probe
Tested MiniMax API directly (chatcompletion_v2 with the stored key):
  HTTP 200 in 0.4s
  BODY: {"base_resp":{"status_code":2049,"status_msg":"invalid api key"}}

### CRITICAL: MiniMax returns HTTP 200 for AUTH FAILURE
MiniMax buries the error in the response BODY (base_resp.status_code=2049, "invalid api key") instead of returning a proper HTTP 401. This is why:
- pi's HTTP layer sees 200 OK -> thinks request succeeded
- parses body -> finds no content/choices -> records empty turn
- user sees silent freeze

### This changes the detection design
Detection CANNOT rely on:
  - HTTP status (MiniMax returns 200 even on auth failure)
  - totalTokens===0 alone (necessary but the real signal is in the body)

Detection MUST also parse provider-specific error bodies:
  - MiniMax: response.base_resp.status_code !== 0 -> error (2049=invalid key, etc.)
  - Surface the actual status_msg ("invalid api key") to the user

### Confirmed root cause of user's recurring freeze
- Quota scraper (browser cookie) = valid -> shows 10% (RED HERRING)
- Model API key (~/.pi/agent/auth.json minimax.key) = INVALID/REVOKED -> all calls return 2049
- Two different credentials drifted apart: cookie alive, key dead
- Fix for user: regenerate MiniMax API key at platform.minimax.io, OR use glm-5.2

### The "Login again" message the user asked for -> now exactly right
For MiniMax specifically, when base_resp.status_code=2049 detected:
  "Your MiniMax API key is invalid or revoked. Regenerate it at platform.minimax.io -> API Keys, or switch models with /model."

## CORRECTION to earlier note (was misled by wrong-endpoint probe)
Earlier note said "key is dead, regenerate it." That was WRONG.
- I tested api.minimax.chat (domestic CN) -> "invalid api key" (red herring)
- pi ACTUALLY uses api.minimax.io/anthropic (Anthropic Messages format)
- Direct probe of the CORRECT endpoint: HTTP 200, valid MiniMax-M2.7 response (input 42, output 5 tokens). KEY IS VALID.

### Revised root cause
Most likely a TRANSIENT MiniMax provider issue (capacity/load during peak Beijing afternoon ~15:00 CST) that returned empty responses for a sustained window (~20+ min), then recovered. User's sessions stayed "stuck" because pi kept recording empty turns and never retried cleanly even after MiniMax recovered.

### This STRENGTHENS the cvn fix (doesn't weaken it)
Regardless of whether empties come from: dead key | transient outage | payload rejection | rate limit
-> ALL of them present as empty/zero-token responses that pi silently swallows.
The fix is the same: detect consecutive empties, surface a clear message, and (importantly) AUTO-RETRY after a short backoff so a transient provider hiccup self-heals instead of leaving the session stuck forever.

### Additional requirement added: auto-retry with backoff
On detecting an empty response, before alerting the user:
1. Wait a short backoff (e.g. 2s, then 5s, then 10s)
2. Retry the SAME request up to 2-3 times
3. Only if retries also come back empty -> surface the provider-aware error message
This would have auto-recovered the transient MiniMax window without any user intervention.

title: Fix auto-resume after compaction and output token limit
Output-limit resume should detect truncated Write and append, not rewrite
description:
## Problem
When the model hits the output token limit mid-`Write` (large file), the harness correctly fires output-limit resume ("continue"). But "continue" makes the model RE-ATTEMPT THE WHOLE FILE from scratch -> same output cap hit -> loop until the 3-attempt circuit breaker stops it -> agent freezes with a truncated file on disk.

Confirmed in production (glm-5.2, 683-line file): the Write tool received cut-off content, file landed with parse errors at the truncation boundary, then looped until cap.

The Role Model's approach: detect that the failed turn was a Write, and instruct continuation to APPEND the remainder, not rewrite.

## Fix
In the output-limit resume path (index.ts message_end handler), detect when the interrupted message contained a Write tool call, and craft a resume prompt that:
1. Tells the model the file was truncated at line N
2. Instructs it to append the REMAINING content via edit (not rewrite the whole file)
3. Optionally: auto-detect truncation by checking if the written file's last line is incomplete (no closing brace / dangling syntax) and pass that info

```ts
const OUTPUT_LIMIT_WRITE_RESUME_PROMPT =
  "Output limit hit mid-Write. The file was truncated. " +
  "Do NOT rewrite the whole file. Use edit/append to add ONLY the remaining content from where it cut off. " +
  "If unsure where it cut off, read the file tail first.";
```

## Detection heuristic
- Check last assistant message content blocks for `type === "tool_use"` with `name === "Write"` or `"edit"` (insert)
- If found, use the write-specific resume prompt instead of the generic OUTPUT_LIMIT_RESUME_PROMPT

## Files
- EDIT: `proactive-compact.ts` (add OUTPUT_LIMIT_WRITE_RESUME_PROMPT + detection helper)
- EDIT: `index.ts` (message_end handler: choose resume prompt based on truncated-tool detection)

## Acceptance
- Large Write that truncates -> resume uses append strategy, not full rewrite
- No infinite loop on repeated truncation (still bounded by 3-attempt cap)
- Small writes unaffected (use generic resume prompt)
- Depends on: pi-harness-runtime-r53 (tests cover the detection branch)

title: Add pre-compaction custom instructions support in pi core
description: Pi core session_before_compact exposes customInstructions in the event, but SessionBeforeCompactResult only supports cancel/compaction and current agent-session.js does not pass extension-provided instructions into compact(). Add core support so extensions can preserve task context for reason=overflow compactions without replacing the whole summary.

title: Inject task-preservation instructions into overflow compaction
description:
## Problem
The PROACTIVE compact passes excellent customInstructions ("Preserve the current task, recent code changes, pending work, exact next step..."). But when pi-coding-agent's core triggers reason="overflow" on its own (the 413 / prompt-too-long path), the harness never injects instructions — overflow compactions get DEFAULT summarization, which loses task continuity.

Overflow is the worst time to lose context: it's mid-task, mid-tool-execution.

The Role Model's reactive path preserves task context via recompaction metadata.

## Fix
Add a session_before_compact listener (confirmed: NONE exists today — grep session_before_compact index.ts returns empty):

```ts
pi.on("session_before_compact", (event, ctx) => {
  if (event.reason === "overflow" || event.reason === "threshold") {
    event.customInstructions =
      "This compaction was triggered by context overflow mid-task. " +
      "Preserve: the current task, exact next step, recent code edits, " +
      "any unresolved errors, and pending tool operations. " +
      "After compaction, continue seamlessly without asking the user to resume.";
  }
});
```

## Verify first
Check whether SessionBeforeCompactEvent.customInstructions is settable from an extension (type shows optional; may be read-only or require a different mutation path via `preparation`). If read-only, use whatever before-phase override the core supports.

## Files
- EDIT: `index.ts` (new pi.on("session_before_compact", ...) handler)
- REF: node_modules/@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent/dist/core/extensions/types.d.ts (SessionBeforeCompactEvent)

## Acceptance
- On real context overflow, compaction summary preserves the in-flight task + next step
- Manual compact unaffected
- Biggest quality win — closes the gap vs the Role Model's reactive context preservation
close_reason: Not implementable in harness with current core API; superseded by pi-harness-runtime-zf0

title: Manual /compact triggers unwanted auto-resume (UX bug)
description:
## Bug
A user-initiated `/compact` (pause-to-think) gets auto-resumed by the harness, so the agent keeps going against the user's intent.

## Root cause
`index.ts:683` session_compact handler calls `shouldQueuePostCompactionResume(event, ...)` which returns true whenever `willRetry !== true`. A MANUAL compact has `willRetry=false` and no pending turn -> harness injects "resume" -> agent continues.

Reference agents (Role Model) NEVER auto-continue a user-initiated compact.

Confirmed: zero uses of `event.reason` in the harness today.

## Fix
Use the `reason: "manual" | "threshold" | "overflow"` field the core already provides:

In `proactive-compact.ts` shouldQueuePostCompactionResume:
```ts
export function shouldQueuePostCompactionResume(
  event: { willRetry?: boolean; reason?: "manual" | "threshold" | "overflow" },
  hasPendingMessages: boolean,
  options?: { force?: boolean },
): boolean {
  // Never auto-resume a manual compact — the user is in control.
  if (event.reason === "manual" && !options?.force) return false;
  return (options?.force === true || event.willRetry !== true) && !hasPendingMessages;
}
```

Then update `index.ts:683` session_compact handler to pass `event.reason` through (currently dropped).

## Files
- EDIT: `proactive-compact.ts` (shouldQueuePostCompactionResume signature + body)
- EDIT: `index.ts:683` (pass event.reason)

## Acceptance
- `/compact` typed manually -> agent does NOT auto-resume, waits for user
- reason="overflow" with willRetry=false -> still auto-resumes (regression guard)
- reason="threshold" -> still auto-resumes
- Depends on: pi-harness-runtime-r53 (tests must cover this case)
close_reason: Manual compact now blocks post-compaction auto-resume unless forced

title: Add tests for proactive-compact gate functions
description:
## Problem
`proactive-compact.ts` exports 4 pure gate functions that control ALL auto-resume behavior, but they have ZERO tests:
- `shouldTriggerProactiveCompact`
- `isOutputLimitAssistantMessage`
- `shouldQueueOutputLimitResume`
- `shouldQueuePostCompactionResume`

These are the single point of failure for "does the agent continue or stall after compaction / output limit". The Role Model's equivalents (threshold/warning-state calculators) are heavily tested.

## Scope
Add `proactive-compact.test.ts` covering:
- Manual compact -> shouldQueuePostCompactionResume returns false (UX bug guard)
- reason="overflow" + willRetry=false -> returns true
- Output-limit at attempt 3 with pending messages -> false
- stopReason: "length" / "max_output_tokens" / "max_tokens" detection
- errorMessage regex fallback ("reached the maximum output token limit")
- threshold gating (percent null -> false, >= 0.9 -> true)

## Why priority 2 / unblocker
The other fixes (manual-compact UX fix, overflow instructions) modify these exact functions. Tests must land first so those changes are safe.

## Files
- NEW: `proactive-compact.test.ts`
- REF: `proactive-compact.ts` (4 exported functions)

## Acceptance
- `bun test proactive-compact` passes
- All gate functions have >=3 cases each (true/false/edge)

title: Add escalating context-usage warning tiers in turn_end
description:
## Problem
Only the always-on footer status + the 90% trigger exist. No escalating amber/red alerts. The Role Model shows warnings at ~83% (threshold - 20k), errors higher.

## Fix
Add a turn_end check (index.ts:676) that surfaces escalating ctx.ui.notify warnings:

```ts
const TIER_WARNING = 0.75;  // amber
const TIER_ERROR = 0.85;    // red
// in turn_end:
const pct = ctx.getContextUsage()?.percent;
if (pct !== null && pct !== undefined) {
  if (pct >= TIER_ERROR) ctx.ui.notify(`Context at ${Math.round(pct)}% — approaching limit`, "warning");
  else if (pct >= TIER_WARNING) ctx.ui.notify(`Context at ${Math.round(pct)}%`, "info");
}
```

Add per-session dedup so it doesn't fire every turn (e.g., only when crossing into a higher tier).

## Files
- EDIT: `index.ts` (turn_end handler)

## Acceptance
- Single amber notify at 75%, single red at 85% (no spam)
- No notify above 90% (proactive compact handles it)
- Polish item — lowest priority
